title: Postal Service Clusterbox Responsibility Act
plain_summary: This bill is about cluster mailbox units, which are groups of mailboxes that serve multiple homes or businesses. If the postal service has been taking care of these mailbox units for at least twenty years, they must keep doing so even if private people or companies own them. The postal service has to fix broken mailboxes, replace old ones, and add new ones when people need them. People can also ask the postal service to officially recognize their cluster mailbox units under this new rule.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Postal Service Clusterbox Responsibility Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Historically Maintained Cluster Box Units
sections.text: This section adds a new rule to the postal laws. The new rule is about cluster box units. A cluster box unit is a group of locked mailboxes that are all together in one spot where the postal service delivers and picks up mail. The postal service must keep taking care of certain cluster box units that they have been taking care of for a long time. Taking care of them means fixing them, replacing them when needed, and adding new mailboxes when necessary. To be covered by this rule, a cluster box unit must be owned by a person or company that is not the government, and the postal service must have been the main ones taking care of it for at least twenty years. Taking care of it mainly means paying for or doing most of the work needed to keep it working, including repairs and replacements. A covered person is someone who can give the postal service permission to take care of a cluster box unit or take that permission away. If someone wants the postal service to officially recognize that their cluster box unit should be covered by this rule, they must send an application. The application must include proof that the postal service has been mainly taking care of the cluster box unit for at least twenty years, written permission for the postal service to keep taking care of it, and any other information the postal service wants. The postal service must look into their records and other information they can reasonably get to help make this decision. They must decide within one month of getting the application and tell the person who applied. If someone needs an extra mailbox added to their covered cluster box unit, they can ask the postal service. The postal service will add the mailbox if they think it is needed. If someone adds a mailbox themselves following all the rules and laws, the postal service must still take care of the whole cluster box unit including the new mailbox. People can still take care of their own cluster box units if they want to. The postal service does not have to do work that is not needed for the cluster box unit to work properly, except they can make repairs that are part of other necessary work. They do not have to fix damage that was done on purpose by the owner or residents. They also do not have to work on building structures. If someone else is legally responsible for paying for repairs because they caused damage, the postal service can ask the person who benefits from the cluster box unit to help them get money from the person who caused the damage. A cluster box unit stops being covered by this rule if the owner takes away permission for the postal service to take care of it, if someone refuses to help the postal service get money from people who cause damage, or if the postal service permanently stops delivering mail to all the addresses that use that cluster box unit. To take away permission, the owner must send a written notice to the postal service. The government will create a special fund called the Cluster Box Unit Maintenance Fund to pay for this work. Money put into this fund can be used by the postal service without yearly limits to carry out this rule. Money given to the postal service by Congress for this purpose will go into this fund. Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the postal service must create detailed rules about how to follow this new law.
